ucs.title: [LLM] Agonizing Serpens (Agrius) web shell drop after Fortinet/Exchange exploitation
ucs.description: Hunts Agrius's signature initial-access chain: exploitation of internet-facing FortiOS/Exchange/Log4j followed by ASPXSpy or 'AntSword'-style web shell deployment to maintain footholds on Israeli/MENA targets. Catches w3wp.exe (or FortiOS web daemons) writing ASPX/JSP/PHP web shells and immediately spawning command interpreters — the precursor to credential theft and wiper staging that defines this MOIS-linked crew (G1030 / Pink Sandstorm / AMERICIUM / BlackShadow).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat*.exe","java.exe") AND (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\*","*\\Exchange Server\\*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*","*\\ClientAccess\\owa\\auth\\*","*\\webapps\\*")) AND Filesystem.file_name IN ("*.aspx","*.ashx","*.asmx","*.jsp","*.jspx","*.php") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` 
| join type=inner process_guid [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","java.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe") by Processes.process_guid Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| table firstTime lastTime dest parent_process_name file_path file_name process_name process
ucs.kql:
let webshell_writes = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\", @"\Exchange Server\", @"FrontEnd\HttpProxy\", @"ClientAccess\owa\auth\", @"\webapps\")
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx" or FileName endswith ".jsp" or FileName endswith ".jspx" or FileName endswith ".php"
| project DeviceId, DeviceName, FileWriteTime=Timestamp, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId;
let child_exec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","java.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","certutil.exe","wmic.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe")
| project DeviceId, ChildExecTime=Timestamp, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessId;
webshell_writes
| join kind=inner child_exec on DeviceId, InitiatingProcessId
| where abs(datetime_diff('minute', ChildExecTime, FileWriteTime)) <= 60
| project FileWriteTime, ChildExecTime, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ChildProcess=FileName1, ProcessCommandLine
| sort by FileWriteTime desc
ucs.rationale: Agrius's public intrusions (Israeli logistics, IT, government via Agonizing Serpens 2023; BlackShadow against insurers; Pink Sandstorm against universities) consistently begin with exploitation of internet-facing FortiOS / Microsoft Exchange (ProxyShell) / Log4j followed by web shell drops (ASPXSpy variants, AntSword) used as the staging beachhead before Mimikatz, SoftPerfect scanner, and wiper de

ucs.title: [LLM] Agrius wiper/ransomware staging: WinRAR archival + service install of unsigned destructive payload
ucs.description: Hunts the late-stage Agrius chain that distinguishes it from generic ransomware crews: heavy data archival via WinRAR (rar.exe) for exfiltration immediately followed by installation of an unsigned binary as a Windows service from a non-standard path — the deployment pattern of Apostle, Fantasy, DEADWOOD, Moneybird, IPsec Helper, and BiBi-Wiper. Disguising wipers as ransomware is an Agrius signature, so this catches the destructive payoff specifically rather than any commodity encryptor.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rar.exe","WinRAR.exe") AND (Processes.process="*a *" OR Processes.process="* -hp*" OR Processes.process="* -m*" OR Processes.process="* -v*" OR Processes.process="* -r *")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_guid 
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` 
| rename dest as host 
| join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true count as svc_count values(Processes.process) as svc_cmd values(Processes.process_path) as svc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("sc.exe","services.exe") AND (Processes.process="*create*" OR Processes.process="*config*") AND (Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host] 
| where lastTime - firstTime < 14400 
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` 
| table firstTime lastTime host user parent cmd svc_cmd
ucs.kql:
let archival = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName in~ ("rar.exe","WinRAR.exe","7z.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" a ", " -hp", " -m5", " -v", " -r ")
| project ArchiveTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=AccountUpn, ArchiveCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let svc_install = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where (FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("create","config")) or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "services.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Temp\", @"C:\Intel\")
| project SvcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, SvcCmd=ProcessCommandLine;
let susp_binary = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\PerfLogs\")
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
| where isempty(Signer) or SignatureType == "Unsigned" or InitiatingProcessAccountName !in ("system","trustedinstaller")
| project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, FolderPath, FileName, SHA256, Signer, SignatureType;
archival
| join kind=inner svc_install on DeviceId
| where datetime_diff('minute', SvcTime, ArchiveTime) between (0 .. 720)
| join kind=leftouter susp_binary on DeviceId
| project ArchiveTime, SvcTime, DropTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ArchiveCmd, SvcCmd, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, Signer
| sort by SvcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Across SentinelOne (Apostle/DEADWOOD), CheckPoint (Fantasy/Moneybird), and Unit 42 (Agonizing Serpens / BiBi-Wiper) reporting, Agrius reliably (1) collects and archives victim data with WinRAR before the destructive payload runs — separating it from pure crypto-ransomware actors — and (2) deploys the wiper masquerading as a ransomware binary via sc.exe service install from world-writable directori
